hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric is incorporated as a physics-informed regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force neural network potentials—penalizing the magnitude of U∆on unlabeled structures—then the resulting models will exhibit simultaneously reduced true force errors and improved calibration of the Force Delta as a quantitative error predictor (i.e., a more linear relationship between U∆and ε_direct), because enforcing internal conservative consistency during training will constrain the model toward physically realizable regions of the potential energy surface, thereby coupling the reduction of non-conservativity with the reduction of epistemic error.
